question.text: An importer buys 100 lots of USDINR futures at 83.40 to hedge. At expiry, the spot is 84.10. What is the futures P&L and what rate does the importer effectively pay?

answer.text: Futures profit of Rs. 70,000; effective rate approximately Rs. 83.40 per USD
Futures loss of Rs. 70,000; effective rate Rs. 84.10 per USD
Futures profit of Rs. 7,000; effective rate Rs. 83.40 per USD
No profit or loss as hedge neutralizes everything

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Futures P&L = (84.10 - 83.40) × 1000 × 100 = Rs. 70,000 profit. The importer buys USD at spot (84.10) but gains Rs. 70,000 from futures. Net cost = (84.10 × 100,000) - 70,000 = Rs. 84,10,000 - 70,000 = Rs. 83,40,000. Effective rate = Rs. 83.40/USD. **The Trap:** The hedge works! Futures profit offsets the higher spot rate, locking in approximately the entry rate. **Cross-Exam Context:** Hedging effectiveness is tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The hedge locks in the futures entry rate regardless of spot movement — that's the purpose of hedging.
